title: Anchor Panels
description: Reference panels with measured parameters for visual drift detection. Produced by Visual Identity Agent (§6.3). Consumed by QC Agent for Level 3 rendered output validation.

style_bible_version.description: The style_bible version these anchor panels were generated against. All drift checks must use anchor panels matching the current style_bible_version.

anchor_panel_id.description: Unique ID for this anchor panel. All downstream agents reference by ID only — free-text names prohibited (Visual Identity Spec §10).

category.description: Panel mood/function category. QC drift detection matches rendered panels to the nearest anchor by category.

reference_image_id.description: File reference or asset ID for the anchor image stored in series/anchor_panels/.

embedding_id.description: ID of the stored embedding vector for similarity comparison during drift detection.

prompt_used.description: The exact generation prompt that produced this anchor panel. Stored for reproducibility.

contrast_ratio.description: Measured contrast ratio of the panel. QC drift threshold: ±0.10.

linework_weight_px.description: Average linework weight in pixels. QC drift threshold: ±0.5px.

shadow_coverage_pct.description: Fraction of panel area in shadow. QC drift threshold: ±0.08.

highlight_coverage_pct.description: Fraction of panel area in highlight. QC drift threshold: ±0.10.

detail_density.description: Detail density tier. Drift = one_tier tolerance (low→medium OK, low→high = drift).

color_temperature.description: Color temperature classification for the panel.

screentone_density_pct.description: Fraction of panel area using screentone. Per Visual Identity Spec §3.5 silence grammar.

model.description: Image generation model used (e.g. flux_v1, sdxl_v1).

lora_ids.description: LoRA model IDs applied during generation.

drift_detection_config.description: Drift detection settings. Per Visual Identity Spec §6.4: check every 10 panels against nearest anchor.

check_interval.description: Check rendered panels against anchors every N panels.

detail_density.description: Tolerance for detail_density drift. 'one_tier' means low→medium OK, low→high = drift.
